enriched_metadata.objective: Predict the onset of diabetes mellitus based on diagnostic measurements in female Pima Indians aged 21 and older
enriched_metadata.sources: National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases - Original study from 1988
enriched_metadata.features_description: Eight medical predictor variables: pregnancies count, glucose concentration, blood pressure, skin thickness, insulin level, BMI, diabetes pedigree function, and age
enriched_metadata.representativity_description: Female Pima Indians aged 21 and older from Arizona with complete medical diagnostic measurements; limited to specific ethnic and geographic population
enriched_metadata.sample_balance_description: Imbalanced dataset with approximately 35% positive diabetes cases (268) and 65% negative cases (500), reflecting natural disease prevalence
enriched_metadata.missing_values_description: Missing values encoded as zeros in several medical measurements (glucose, blood pressure, skin thickness, insulin, BMI) which is medically impossible
enriched_metadata.data_errors_description: Missing values incorrectly encoded as zeros for medical measurements; requires careful preprocessing to handle medically impossible zero values
